lore_sections.title: Vòng Lặp Nhân-Quả: The Hollow Sun's Cosmic Timer
lore_sections.content_summary: Cosmological framework explaining the 3-year death cycle, the Hollow Sun's nature, and why the living must witness the dead. Establishes nước mắm cốt mechanics as spiritual necessity.
mechanics_grounded: 3-year cycle (1,095 days)
nước mắm cốt (15-20 min protection mechanic)
shrine safety zones (reduced sun damage)
daily stall management (day phase)
Hollow Sun daily rise/set cycle

lore_sections.title: Cá Ông: From Guardian to Ghost
lore_sections.title_vn: Cá Ông Bộ Xương - Người Giữ Biên Giới
lore_sections.content_summary: Origin story of the boss creature: Cá Ông as corrupted guardian bound to beach by monks. Driven by hunger and desperation rather than malice. Hints at secret redemption through true-name ritual.
mechanics_grounded: Boss encounter (night survival run)
Boundary failure consequences
Spiritual hunger mechanics
Guardian role (failed threshold-guardian)
Seekable redemption path (true name: Cá Vương Tử Thúy)

easter_egg.hidden_condition: Player speaks true name 'Cá Vương Tử Thúy' during boss encounter
easter_egg.outcome: Boss immediately de-aggros, allows passage without combat
easter_egg.requires_knowledge: Vietnamese cosmology, shrine records, or deep lore exploration

themes: loss of identity through lost names

lore_sections.title: Tú's Accidental Awakening: The Night of the Storm
lore_sections.content_summary: Ambiguous origin story of Tú as threshold-birth accident. Three narratives (what Tú knows vs. village whispers vs. truth) create intentional ambiguity about Tú's existential status. Explains natural empathy with dead.
lore_sections.player_visibility: Discoverable (textual hints throughout, never explicitly confirmed)
mechanics_grounded: Player-as-threshold-being (explains why Tú can interact with ghosts naturally)
Permanent daytime awareness (Tú never sleeps normally)
Natural spiritual empathy (no training montage needed)
Involuntary stall management (compulsion to serve)
No shadow casting (environmental visual cue—Tú's form is semi-solid)
lore_sections.narrative_design_philosophy: Intentional ambiguity is a core feature, not a bug. Players should gather circumstantial evidence of Tú's threshold nature (no shadow, old woman's sadness, ghost behavior) without explicit confirmation. Ambiguity is more powerful than answers.
evidence_for_players_to_find: Old woman's trembling hands when looking at Tú
Ông Văn's whispered prayers thanking that Tú 'never leaves'
Shrine records mentioning 'threshold birth, 23 years prior'
Tú casts almost no shadow in high sun
Ghosts sometimes move through Tú's physical space as if Tú were less solid

lore_sections.title: The Three Keepers: Linh, Văn, Minh
lore_sections.title_vn: Ba Người Giữ Đền Thờ: Linh, Văn, Minh
lore_sections.content_summary: Individual backstories of the three main NPCs as shrine keepers with distinct roles: Văn (memory), Linh (witness), Minh (boundary). Establishes their traumas, keeper functions, and emotional arcs.
lore_sections.player_visibility: Discoverable (revealed through repeated dialogue and shrine records)
source_dependencies: SOLAR_PHOBIA_CHARACTER_SHEETS.md (Ông Văn, Em Linh, Anh Minh sections)
mechanics_grounded: Three NPC slots (stall capacity during day phase: max 3 customers)
Character arc progression (early visits → mid-game → late-game arc completion)
Dialogue branching (player choices affect emotional depth)
Stall-of-witness narrative framing (orders are prayers, service is witnessing)
Spirit Essence rewards (tied to emotional arc completion)

ong_van.cause_of_death: Drowned retrieving lost fishing nets in storm
ong_van.family_status: Widow (now remarried), two adult children (one in Saigon, one abroad)
ong_van.bound_to_shrine_by: Practical love for family and sea; children never paid respects
ong_van.keeper_function: Reminds other ghosts of their pre-death identity; anchor of memory
ong_van.visual_design: Weathered brown shirt, missing two fingers (left hand), burn marks, limping gait
ong_van.dialogue_pattern: Fishing metaphors, regional dialect, contemplative pauses
ong_van.reward_for_compassion: Peace; clarity that 'sea is not cruel, they forgot how to listen'

em_linh.cause_of_death: Drowned swimming with friends; body never found
em_linh.family_status: Parents performed funeral rites but cannot let go; grief holds her
em_linh.keeper_function: Sees and names other ghosts; asks questions that matter; emotional center
em_linh.visual_design: Blue-gray pallor, wet black hair, dripping áo dài, trails water droplets
em_linh.dialogue_pattern: Soft-spoken, apologetic, curious observations about nature
em_linh.second_order: Rice (to feed the fish, so they're not lonely)
em_linh.reward_for_compassion: Asks player to sit by water at shift's end; opens about memory fading

anh_minh.death_date: Walked into sea in 1978 (approx 49 years prior); lived 1945-1978
anh_minh.cause_of_death: Suicide by walking into sea; wearing wedding áo dài
anh_minh.family_status: Engaged to Hùng; fiancé conscripted and fled to South during Fall of Saigon (1975); never returned
anh_minh.bound_to_shrine_by: Paradox of waiting in two times simultaneously (1975 and present)
anh_minh.keeper_function: Guards shrine boundaries; reminds Tú of time constraints; understands endings
anh_minh.personality_arc: Hostile → Vulnerability slip (mentioning Hùng) → Deep confession and gratitude
anh_minh.visual_design: Torn white-and-gold wedding áo dài, rust stains, severe beauty, flickering form (unstable emotions)
anh_minh.dialogue_pattern: Bitter humor, sarcasm, sharp critiques; rage masking loneliness
anh_minh.first_order: Strong wine + large pile of mock money (for shrine building)
anh_minh.second_order: Quiet time; needs to talk instead of be served
anh_minh.reward_for_compassion: Confession about Hùng; cold-hand grip; thanks for being seen; urges Tú to continue work

keeper_bond.description: The three keepers know each other, bound by shared shrine duty
keeper_bond.dynamic: Not friends, but deeply interdependent; they share the burden of tending
keeper_bond.benefit_of_player_witness: As Tú serves them with compassion, their forms stabilize, colors become vivid, full stories emerge
keeper_bond.consequence_of_neglect: Forms glitch, colors fade, distortion increases; they begin to degrade spiritually

lore_sections.title: Why Only Two Souls: The Spiritual Limit
lore_sections.title_vn: Tại Sao Chỉ Có Hai Linh Hồn
lore_sections.content_summary: Cosmological hard limit: exactly two threshold-beings can exist at any time. One slot = Tú (permanent guardian). One slot = rotating visitor (current cycle's most urgent soul). Explores the moral trap of endless compassion and Tú's binding.
lore_sections.player_visibility: Hidden (discoverable only through deep play, shrine records, and careful observation)
mechanics_grounded: Two-soul threshold capacity (hard limit in spiritual system)
Tú occupies slot 1 permanently (cannot ascend without catastrophe)
Visitor occupies slot 2 per cycle (current urgent soul needing witness)
Corruption consequence if visitor unwitnessed (soul becomes hunger, pulls others down)
Boundary collapse if Tú ascends (no new guardian born in time)
Three-year cycle repetition (each cycle requires new witness from slot 2)

tú_binding.description: Tú cannot ascend without catastrophic consequences
consequences_if_tú_ascends: New guardian must be born (another threshold birth)
Living begin to see masses of spirits
tú_binding.practical_implication: Tú is imprisoned by compassion; freedom means damnation for millions

visitor_slot.description: One soul per cycle occupies threshold slot 2
visitor_slot.selection_criteria: Most spiritually damaged; most unwitnessed; closest to corruption
visitor_slot.current_cycle_visitor: TBD (game discovers which of the three keepers is most urgent)
visitor_slot.player_choice_impact: Serving visitor with compassion stabilizes them; neglect allows corruption

player_discovery_breadcrumbs: Old woman's resigned expression when explaining shrine duty
Ông Văn's quiet prayer: 'Please never leave us'
Shrine records mentioning 'two souls bound, endless cycle'
Em Linh asking 'Will you come back? I don't want to be alone'
Anh Minh's comment: 'You serve us like you have no choice'
Realization during final sequence that Tú cannot leave at end of Act 1
themes: the cost of being a bridge
lore_sections.narrative_weight: This is the game's deepest tragedy: not that the dead are trapped, but that the living who choose to tend them become trapped too. Tú must choose every day to stay. Or the dead stay forgotten.

metadata.narrative_scope: Foundational cosmology + character traumas + protagonist binding + spiritual mechanics
metadata.canon_status: All sections Established or Provisional (intentional ambiguity in Tú's section)
metadata.consistency_notes: All 5 sections cross-reference each other. Mechanics are grounded in approved GDDs. Vietnamese terminology preserved with English glosses. No contradictions detected.
